title: javascript part 2
content:
📘 DOM + JavaScript Notes (Hinglish) — Part 1

🔹 DOM kya hota hai? (Basic Understanding)

👉 DOM = Document Object Model

Simple language me:

  • Jo bhi HTML page tum banate ho

  • Wo objects ka structure hota hai

Example:

  • <html> → object

  • <head> → object

  • <body> → object

  • <div> → object

👉 Ye sab milke ek tree structure banate hain
👉 Isi ko bolte hain DOM

🔹 Important Concept

👉 “Agar tum DOM samajh gaye”:

To tum:

  • Data read kar sakte ho

  • Value change kar sakte ho

  • New elements add kar sakte ho

  • Elements delete kar sakte h

🔹 Window Object (Sabse Top Level)

👉 Browser me sab kuch ek global object ke andar hota hai:

  • Uska naam hai → window

💡 Simple samjho:

  • Browser = Window

  • Sab kuch uske andar hai

🔹 Structure Start hota hai yahan se

Hierarchy:

Window
  ↓
Document
  ↓
HTML
  ↓
Head + Body

🔹 Window ke andar kya hota hai?

Jab tum console me likhte ho:

console.log(window)

👉 Tumhe bahut saari cheezein dikhti hain:

  • location (tum kaha ho)

  • history

  • navigator

  • document

👉 Ye sab window ke properties hain

🔹 Important: document

👉 Sabse important object hai:

document

👉 Ye represent karta hai:

  • Tumhara pura HTML page

🔹 Shortcut Understanding

  • window.document ✔️

  • document ✔️ (direct use kar sakte ho)

👉 Kyun?

  • Kyunki browser automatically samajhta hai ki tum document use karoge

🔹 Document ke andar kya hota hai?

console.log(document)

👉 Tumhe milta hai:

  • doctype

  • html

  • head

  • body

👉 Ye hi actual DOM structure hai

🔹 DOM Tree ka Concept (Very Important 🔥)

👉 DOM ek tree structure hota hai

Structure:

Window
  ↓
Document
  ↓
HTML
  ├── Head
  │     ├── Title
  │     │     └── Text Node
  │     └── Meta (attributes only)
  │
  └── Body
        └── Div
              ├── H1
              │     ├── Attribute (Class name)
              │     └── Text Node
              │
              └── Paragraph
                    └── Text Node

🔹 Node kya hota hai?

👉 DOM me har cheez ko Node bola jata hai

Types:

  1. Element Node
    → <div>, <h1>, <p>

  2. Text Node
    → Jo text likha hai

  3. Attribute Node
    → class, id

🔹 Example Breakdown

<title>DOM Learning</title>

  • Title → Element Node

  • “DOM Learning” → Text Node

🔹 Parent-Child Concept

Example:

  • Div → Parent

  • H1 & P → Children

👉 Important for DOM navigation

🔹 Sibling Concept

Example:

  • H1 aur P
    👉 Dono same level pe hain → siblings

🔹 Real Use Case

Agar tumhe kisi element tak jana hai:

👉 Tumhe path pata hona chahiye:

window → document → html → body → div → p

🔹 Selection ka logic

Agar multiple div ho:

👉 Tumhe identify karna padega:

  • class

  • id

  • attribute

📘 DOM + JavaScript Notes (Hinglish) — Part 3

🔹 Real Browser me DOM kaise dekhein?

👉 Kisi bhi website pe jao
👉 Right click → Inspect

👉 Fir open karo:

  • Console tab

Observation

👉 Jab tum window open karte ho:

  • Bahut zyada data hota hai

👉 Jab tum document open karte ho:

  • Focused HTML structure milta hai

🔹 Advanced Console Methods 

DOCUMENT PURE PAGE KO PKD KE RKHTA H 

Normal log:

console.log(document)

👉 Limited view deta hai

Better debugging:

console.dir(document)

👉 Full properties show karta hai

🔹 Useful Properties

1. Base URL

document.baseURI

👉 Current page ka URL deta hai

2. Links Collection

document.links

👉 Page ke saare links deta hai

🔹 Important Concept ⚠️

👉 document.links kya return karta hai?

  • ❌ Array nahi

  • ✅ HTMLCollection

🔹 HTMLCollection vs Array

HTMLCollection = live collection (auto update hota hai)

👉 Difference:

FeatureHTMLCollectionArray
map()❌✅
forEach()❌✅
index access✅✅

🔹 Example

document.links[0]

👉 First link mil jayega

🔹 Conversion Trick

👉 Agar array banana ho:

Array.from(document.links)

👉 Ab tum map, filter use kar sakte ho

🔹 Element Select kaise karein?

👉 DOM ka main kaam = elements select karna

🔹 Method 1: getElementById ⭐

document.getElementById("firstHeading")

👉 Ye return karega:

  • pura HTML element

Example output:

<h1 id="firstHeading">...</h1>

🔹 Ab Manipulation start 🔥

👉 Ab hum element change kar sakte hain

🔹 innerHTML (Very Important)

document.getElementById("firstHeading").innerHTML = "Chai aur Code"

👉 Result:

  • Page ka heading change ho jayega

🔹 Kya hua internally?

👉 Tumne:

  • DOM access kiya

  • Element select kiya

  • Text replace kar diya

👉 Isko bolte hain:

👉 DOM Manipulation

🔹 Real Power 💥

Ab tum kar sakte ho:

  • Text change

  • HTML add/remove

  • Elements create

  • Elements delete                                  

🔹 Console Me Kaam Start

  • Browser me Console open kiya

  • Yahin se hum JavaScript DOM ke saath interact karenge

🔹 Element Select Karna (getElementById)

  • Sabse pehla method:

document.getElementById("title")

👉 Breakdown:

  • document → pura HTML page

  • .getElementById() → specific element ko select karega

  • "title" → id jo humne diya tha

⚠️ Important Syntax Note

  • Case sensitive hota hai:

    • getElementById (G, E, I capital)

🔹 Output Kya Mila?

  • Ye method return karta hai:
    👉 poora HTML element (object form me)

🔹 Object Se Data Kaise Nikaale?

  • Dot (.) use karke properties access karte hain

Example:

document.getElementById("title").id

👉 Output: "title"

🔹 Class Access Karna

document.getElementById("title").className

👉 Important:

  • HTML me → class

  • JavaScript me → className

⚠️ Concept Clear

  • JS me directly class nahi likhte

  • Always use:
    👉 className

🔹 getAttribute() Method

  • Kisi bhi attribute ki value lene ke liye:

document.getElementById("title").getAttribute("id")

👉 Output: "title"

document.getElementById("title").getAttribute("class")

👉 Output: "heading"

🔹 setAttribute() Method

  • Attribute change/set karne ke liye

document.getElementById("title").setAttribute("class", "test")

👉 Result:

  • Old class "heading" remove ho jayegi

  • New class "test" set ho jayegi

⚠️ Important Warning

  • setAttribute overwrite karta hai

  • Old value remove ho jaati hai

🔹 Multiple Classes Set Karna

document.getElementById("title").setAttribute("class", "test heading")

👉 Result:

  • Dono classes apply ho jayengi

🔹 Variable Me Store Karna

  • Easy access ke liye:

const title = document.getElementById("title")

👉 Ab baar-baar likhne ki zarurat nahi

🔹 Key Learning

  • Element mil gaya → ab usko:

    • read kar sakte ho

    • modify kar sakte ho

    • attributes change kar sakte ho

📒 Part 3 Notes (Style Manipulation + Styling via JS) – Hinglish

🔹 Element Already Stored

const title = document.getElementById("title")

👉 Ab title ek object hai jiske andar bahut properties hain

🔹 Style Property Access Karna

title.style

👉 Ye ek object hai jisme saari CSS properties hoti hain

🔹 CSS vs JS Syntax Difference

  • CSS me:

background-color

  • JS me:

backgroundColor

👉 camelCase use hota hai JS me

🔹 Background Color Change

title.style.backgroundColor = "green"

👉 Instant UI change ho jayega

🔹 Text Color Change

title.style.color = "white"

🔹 Padding Add Karna

title.style.padding = "15px"

🔹 Border Radius Add Karna

title.style.borderRadius = "10px"

🔹 Important Concept

  • style ek object hai

  • Har CSS property ko:
    👉 .propertyName = "value" se set karte hain

🔹 Live Change Effect

  • Jaise hi code run karte ho:
    👉 Browser me instant change visible

🔹 Multiple Styles Apply Karna

title.style.backgroundColor = "green"
title.style.color = "white"
title.style.padding = "15px"
title.style.borderRadius = "10px"

🔹 Key Learning

  • JS se styling karna = dynamic UI change

  • Ye hi real power hai DOM manipulation ki

📒 Part 4 Notes (Content Manipulation – textContent, innerText, innerHTML) – Hinglish

🔹 Content Nikalna (Text Access)

  • Element ka actual text lene ke liye:

title.textContent

👉 Output: pura text jo element ke andar hai

🔹 Similar Properties (3 Important)

  1. textContent

  2. innerText

  3. innerHTML

👉 Teeno similar lagte hain but difference hai ⚠️

🔸 1. textContent

title.textContent

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • Saara content de deta hai

  • Even hidden content bhi (CSS se hide ho toh bhi)

🔸 2. innerText

title.innerText

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • Sirf visible text deta hai

  • Jo screen pe dikh raha hai wahi milega

🔸 3. innerHTML

title.innerHTML

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • HTML ke saath content deta hai

  • Tags bhi include karta hai

🔹 Example Situation

  • Suppose:

<h1 id="title">
  Hello <span style="display:none;">Hidden</span>
</h1>

🔹 Difference समझो

  • textContent 👉 "Hello Hidden"

  • innerText 👉 "Hello"

  • innerHTML 👉 "Hello <span style='display:none;'>Hidden</span>"

🔹 Key Concept

  • textContent → full data (hidden + visible)

  • innerText → only visible

  • innerHTML → HTML structure bhi

🔹 Content Change Karna

👉 Text change kar sakte ho:

title.innerText = "New Text"

🔹 HTML Inject Karna

title.innerHTML = "<h2>New Heading</h2>"

👉 Ye:

  • pura content replace karega

  • new HTML render karega

⚠️ Important Warning

  • innerHTML dangerous ho sakta hai:

    • agar user input directly daal diya toh
      👉 XSS attack ho sakta hai

🔹 Real Use Cases

  • textContent → safe data fetch

  • innerText → UI-based changes

  • innerHTML → HTML insert karna

✅ Summary (Part 4)

  • 3 main properties samjhi

  • Hidden vs visible difference clear hua

  • Content read + change karna seekha

  • HTML insert karna bhi seekha

📒 Part 5 Notes (Query Selector + Advanced Selection) – Hinglish

🔹 Problem with Old Methods

  • getElementById → sirf id ke liye

  • getElementsByClassName → limited
    👉 Real-world me flexible nahi

  • This can do:

    • multiple classes

    • nested elements

    • attribute selectors

    • pseudo-classes

🔹 Solution: querySelector 🔥

document.querySelector("h1")

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • First matching element return karta hai

🔹 Important Rule

  • Ye hamesha:
    👉 sirf first element select karta hai

🔹 Multiple Elements Example

<h2>One</h2>
<h2>Two</h2>
<h2>Three</h2>
document.querySelector("h2")

👉 Output: "One" (first h2)

🔹 ID Select Karna (CSS Style)

document.querySelector("#title")

👉 # → id selector

🔹 Class Select Karna

document.querySelector(".heading")

👉 . → class selector

🔹 CSS Selectors Use Karna

👉 Same CSS syntax use hota hai:

document.querySelector("div p")

👉 matlab:

  • div ke andar jo p hai → select karo

🔹 Attribute Selector

document.querySelector("input[type='password']")

👉 specific element select kar sakte ho

🔹 querySelectorAll 🔥

document.querySelectorAll("h2")

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • saare matching elements deta hai

🔹 Return Type

  • querySelector → single element

  • querySelectorAll → NodeList

🔹 NodeList kya hota hai?

  • Array jaisa hota hai

  • But exact array nahi hota

🔹 NodeList Access

const items = document.querySelectorAll("h2")

items[0]  // first element
items[1]  // second element

🔹 Loop Use Karna (forEach)

items.forEach(function(item) {
    item.style.color = "red"
})

👉 Saare elements pe style apply ho jayega

🔹 Real Example (List)

const ul = document.querySelector("ul")
const li = ul.querySelector("li")

li.style.backgroundColor = "green"

cont h1  = documentqueryselectorall("h1")
h1 ke nodelist aayegi or saari ko print kar rhe honge 
h1.style.color = 'green'
ause direct allowed nhi hoga likhna we have to do this like
myh1[0].style.color = 'green'
aise krna pdega quese yeh nodelist derha h for each use kar skte ho 

templilist.forEach(fucntion (li) {
li.style.color = 'green'
})

saare ka krdegaa

Array.from(htmlcollection)

kisko bhi arrrayt me conver krdega


🔹 Chain Query (Important)

  • Parent se child select kar sakte ho:

ul.querySelector("li")

🔹 Text Change

li.innerText = "Five"

👉 Content change ho gaya

🔹 Key Learning

  • querySelector = powerful + flexible

  • CSS selectors use kar sakte ho

  • querySelectorAll = multiple elements handle

📒 Part 6 Notes (NodeList vs HTMLCollection + Array Conversion) – Hinglish

🔹 Ab Tak Kya Dekha?

  • querySelectorAll → NodeList देता है

  • getElementsByClassName → HTMLCollection देता है

👉 Dono same nahi hote ⚠️

🔸 NodeList kya hota hai?

const items = document.querySelectorAll("li")

👉 Features:

  • Array jaisa hota hai

  • Index se access kar sakte ho

  • forEach support karta hai ✅

items.forEach(item => {
    item.style.color = "red"
})

🔸 HTMLCollection kya hota hai?

const items = document.getElementsByClassName("list-item")

👉 Features:

  • Array jaisa dikhta hai

  • But:
    ❌ forEach directly support nahi karta
    ❌ map bhi nahi

🔹 Problem Example

items.forEach(item => {
    item.style.color = "green"
})

👉 ❌ Error aayega (HTMLCollection me forEach nahi hota)

🔸 Solution → Convert into Array 🔥

const arr = Array.from(items)

👉 Ab:

  • Pure array ban gaya

  • Saare methods use kar sakte ho

🔹 Loop Ab Kaam Karega

arr.forEach(item => {
    item.style.backgroundColor = "orange"
})

🔸 Important Difference (Simple Language)

FeatureNodeListHTMLCollection
forEach✅ Yes❌ No
map❌ No❌ No
Array jaisa✅✅
Direct loop easy✅❌

🔹 Key Concept

👉 Jab bhi:

  • querySelectorAll → easy (NodeList)

  • getElementsByClassName → convert karna padega

🔸 Real Mistake (Common)

❌ Log directly HTMLCollection pe loop laga dete hain
👉 Error aata hai

✔️ Correct way:

Array.from(collection).forEach(...)

🔹 Example Flow

const tempClassList = document.getElementsByClassName("list-item")

const convertedArray = Array.from(tempClassList)

convertedArray.forEach(li => {
    li.style.color = "blue"
})

🔹 Deep Insight

  • NodeList aur HTMLCollection:
    👉 pure arrays nahi hote

  • Isliye:
    👉 kuch methods missing hote hain

🔹 Best Practice

  • Modern JS me:
    👉 querySelectorAll prefer karo

  • Agar old method use kiya:
    👉 convert to array

📒 Part 7 Notes (Real Website Practice – Wikipedia DOM Manipulation) – Hinglish

🔹 Real Practice Start

  • Ab hum apni file se bahar nikle

  • Real website pe practice ki 👉 Wikipedia

👉 Reason:

  • Real-world DOM structure samajhna

  • Complex elements handle karna

🔹 Inspect Element

  • Kisi bhi heading pe right click → Inspect

  • Example:

    • Heading (h2, h3) select ki

🔹 All Headings Select Karna

document.querySelectorAll("h2")

👉 Output:

  • NodeList of all h2 elements

🔹 Variable Me Store

const headings = document.querySelectorAll("h2")

🔹 First Element Access

headings[0].innerText

👉 Example output:

  • "History"

  • "Trademark"

🔹 Multiple Elements Manipulate Karna

👉 Loop lagate hain:

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.color = "red"
})

👉 Result:

  • Saare headings red ho gaye 🔴

🔹 Background + Padding Add

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.backgroundColor = "green"
    h.style.padding = "10px"
})

🔹 Content Change Karna

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.innerText = "Hitesh"
})

👉 Result:

  • Saare headings ka text change 😄

🔹 Important Observation ⚠️

  • Kabhi extra elements bhi select ho jaate hain
    👉 Kyun?

  • Same tag (h2) multiple jagah use hota hai

🔹 Better Targeting (Class Use)

document.querySelectorAll(".mw-headline")

👉 Ye specific class hai Wikipedia headings ki

🔹 Store + Loop

const heads = document.querySelectorAll(".mw-headline")

heads.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.color = "black"
    h.style.backgroundColor = "green"
    h.style.padding = "5px"
})

🔹 Key Learning

  • Real websites me:
    👉 exact selector choose karna important hai

  • Sirf tag se kaam nahi chalta
    👉 class/id better hota hai

🔹 Powerful Concept

👉 Jo humne console me kiya:

  • Same code hum:

    • <script> tag me likh sakte hain

    • ya external JS file me

🔹 Real Use Case

  • Database se data aaya

  • Loop lagaya

  • Existing elements update kiye

👉 Ye sab same concept se hota hai

🎯 Ab tum kya kar sakte ho:

  • Apni website me dynamic changes

  • Data ko UI me inject karna

  • Basic projects banana

🔹 Topic: Introduction + Parent & Children in DOM

🧠 1. DOM ka basic idea

  • DOM = Document Object Model

  • Ye ek tree structure hota hai (like parent → child → sibling)

  • Har HTML element ek node hota hai

👉 Example structure:

<body>
  <div class="parent">
    <div class="day">Monday</div>
    <div class="day">Tuesday</div>
  </div>
</body>

🧠 2. Parent Element select karna

const parent = document.querySelector('.parent')

✔️ Ye .parent div ko select karega

🧠 3. Children access karna

parent.children

  • Ye return karta hai: HTMLCollection

  • Ye array jaisa hota hai (but exact array nahi)

👉 Example output:

HTMLCollection(4) [div.day, div.day, div.day, div.day]

🧠 4. Children count (length)

parent.children.length

✔️ Kitne child elements hain → return karega

🧠 5. Specific child access karna

parent.children[0]

✔️ First child (Monday)

parent.children[1].innerHTML

✔️ Output: "Tuesday"

🧠 6. Loop through children

for (let i = 0; i < parent.children.length; i++) {
  console.log(parent.children[i].innerHTML)
}

✔️ Output:

Monday
Tuesday
Wednesday
Thursday

🧠 7. Styling child element

parent.children[0].style.color = "orange"

✔️ First element ka color change ho jayega

🧠 8. Important properties

👉 First child element

parent.firstElementChild

✔️ First child return karega //thursfay 

👉 Last child element

parent.lastElementChild

✔️ Last child return karega

🧠 9. Key understanding

  • .children → only element nodes

  • Array jaisa behave karta hai

  • Loop easily chal sakta hai

⚡ Important Concept

👉 DOM traversal ka matlab:

  • Parent → Child

  • Child → Parent

  • Sibling → Next / Previous

Ye sab aage React samajhne me help karega 🚀


🧠 10. Child se Parent pe jaana

Agar aapne koi child element select kiya hai:

const dayOne = document.querySelector('.day')

👉 Ab parent pe jaana ho:

dayOne.parentElement

✔️ Ye uska parent return karega (i.e. .parent div)

⚡ Important point

  • Baar-baar querySelector use karna expensive hota hai

  • Direct relation use karna better hai:

    • parentElement

    • nextElementSibling

🧠 11. Sibling access (Next bhai 😄)

dayOne.nextElementSibling

✔️ Output: "Tuesday"

👉 Matlab:

  • Monday → next sibling → Tuesday

🧠 Related properties

  • nextElementSibling → next element

  • previousElementSibling → previous element

🧠 12. DOM = Tree traversal (DSA connection 🔥)

  • DOM ek tree structure hai

  • Jab aap:

    • parent → child jaate ho

    • child → parent jaate ho

    • sibling access karte ho

👉 basically aap tree traverse kar rahe ho

🧠 13. childNodes (Most confusing but important ⚠️)

parent.childNodes

👉 Ye return karta hai: NodeList

🤯 Surprise:

Agar 4 div hain, fir bhi count zyada aa sakta hai

parent.childNodes.length

👉 Output ho sakta hai: 9 (not 4 😲)

❓ Why?

Kyuki childNodes include karta hai:

  • text nodes (line breaks, spaces)

  • comments

  • elementsjj

🧠 Example:

<div class="parent">
  <div>Monday</div>
  <div>Tuesday</div>
</div>

👉 Browser internally isko count karta hai:

  • line break → text node

  • div → element node

  • line break → text node

🔥 Result:

NodeList(5) [text, div, text, div, text]

🧠 14. NodeList vs HTMLCollection

FeatureHTMLCollectionNodeList
Source.children.childNodes
IncludesOnly elementsEverything (text, comment, element)
Array-likeYesYes
LoopLimitedMore flexible

⚡ Key Difference

👉 .children:

  • clean data (only elements)

👉 .childNodes:

  • raw DOM structure (including spaces, comments)

🧠 15. Important Insight (Interview level 🔥)

  • DOM sirf visible elements nahi hota

  • Hidden nodes bhi exist karte hain:

    • spaces

    • line breaks

    • comments

👉 Isi wajah se:

  • Performance impact hota hai

  • React internally optimize karta hai (Virtual DOM)

🧠 16. Example: Comment bhi node hota hai

<!-- this is comment -->

👉 Ye bhi count hota hai childNodes me

⚡ Experiment

  • Ek extra line break add karo → nodes increase

  • Comment add karo → nodes increase

🧠 17. Summary of Traversal

✔️ Parent → Child

parent.children

✔️ Child → Parent

child.parentElement

✔️ Sibling

child.nextElementSibling

✔️ Raw Nodes

parent.childNodes

🎯 Important Takeaway

👉 DOM = Tree
👉 Har cheez node hai
👉 .children vs .childNodes samajhna very important

🔹 Topic: Creating Elements in DOM (createElement + attributes)

🧠 18. New Element create karna

👉 DOM me new element banane ke liye:

const div = document.createElement('div')

✔️ Ye ek new div create karega (memory me)
❌ Abhi page pe visible nahi hoga

⚡ Important

👉 Element sirf memory me create hota hai
👉 Page pe tab dikhega jab usko attach karenge

🧠 19. Check element (inspect)

Agar console me dekho:

console.log(div)

✔️ Output: <div></div>

🧠 20. Class add karna

div.className = "main"

✔️ Class add ho gayi

🧠 21. ID add karna

div.id = "myId"

🎲 Random ID (fun concept 😄)

div.id = Math.round(Math.random() * 10 + 1)

✔️ Har refresh pe new ID generate hogi

🧠 22. Custom Attribute add karna

div.setAttribute("title", "generated title")

✔️ Title attribute add ho gaya

⚡ Difference

  • div.id = ... → direct property

  • setAttribute() → generic method (recommended for custom attributes)

🧠 23. Styling add karna


div.style.backgroundColor = "green"
div.style.padding = "10px"

//abhi tk kuch bhi page pe nhi dikh rha hoga

✔️ CSS JS se apply ho gaya

🧠 24. Text add karna (Method 1 ❌ less optimal)

div.innerText = "Chai aur Code"

⚠️ Problem:

  • Ye pehle old value fetch karta hai

  • Fir overwrite karta hai

👉 Slightly inefficient // ek roundtrip extra h 

🧠 25. Text add karna (Method 2 ✅ better)

which is preferable

📘 JavaScript Async (Asynchronous Code) – Notes (Part 1)

🔹 1. Video Overview

  • ये पूरा topic theoretical है
  • Goal:
    • Async code समझना
    • Foundation build करना
    • आगे Promises, async/await आसानी से समझ आ जाए

🔹 2. JavaScript Basics (Very Important)

✅ JavaScript by default:

  • Synchronous होती है (line by line) (ek ke baad ek )
  • Single-threaded होती है (slow kh skte ho) ( but dont feel like slow)

🔸 Synchronous क्या होता है?

  • Code line-by-line execute होता है
  • Next line तब तक execute नहीं होती जब तक previous complete न हो जाए

👉 Example:

console.log(1);
console.log(2);
console.log(3);

➡ Output: 1 → 2 → 3 (sequence maintain)

🔸 Single-threaded क्या होता है?

  • JavaScript में only 1 thread होता है
  • मतलब:
    • एक समय पर सिर्फ 1 task execute होगा

❗ Important Point:

  • JS slow लग सकती है compared to multi-threaded languages
  • BUT:
    • JS smart है क्योंकि ये delegation use करती है
    • यानी कुछ काम बाहर भेज देती है (browser / runtime को)

🔹 3. Runtime Environment

JavaScript अकेले काम नहीं करती ❌
हमेशा किसी environment में चलती है:

🔸 Examples:

  • Browser
  • Node.js

👉 यहाँ extra features मिलते हैं:

  • Web APIs (browser में)
  • APIs (Node.js में)

🔹 4. Execution Context (Short Recap)

जब JS code चलता है:

  • Execution context बनता है

इसमें होता है:

  1. Memory Heap → variables store होते हैं
  2. Call Stack → functions execute होते हैं

🔸 Call Stack कैसे काम करता है?

  • Stack structure (LIFO - Last In First Out)

👉 Example:

main()
  -> func1()
     -> func2()

Execution:

  • func2 → func1 → main

🔸 Important Rule:

👉 Each operation waits for previous one to complete

➡ यही synchronous behavior है

🔹 5. Blocking vs Non-Blocking Code

🔸 Blocking Code

  • Program execution रुक जाता है
  • जब तक task complete न हो जाए

👉 Real-life example:

  • तुमसे कहा: “बैठे रहो, कुछ मत करो”
    ➡ तुम stuck हो गए

🔸 Non-Blocking Code

  • Program रुकता नहीं है
  • बाकी काम चलता रहता है

👉 Example:

  • “तुम अपना काम करो, मैं पानी लेकर आता हूँ”

🔹 6. Real Problem (File Reading)

❗ Biggest blocking task:

  • File read करना

क्यों?

  • Direct access नहीं मिलता
  • OS (Operating System) involve होता है

Flow:

  1. Program → request भेजता है
  2. OS kernel → file read करता है
  3. Response वापस देता है

➡ तब तक program wait करता है (blocking)

jb tk program hold hojata h kuch or kar nhi skta 

🔹 7. Sync vs Async File Reading

🔸 Sync (Blocking)

  • File read होने तक program रुक जाएगा

🔸 Async (Non-blocking)

  • Program चलता रहेगा ( baaki ka kaam hota rhega )
  • File ready होने पर callback मिलेगा

🔹 8. Important Insight ⚠️

❌ "Non-blocking हमेशा best है" → WRONG (humesha best nahi h )
✅ "Depends on use-case"
in case of login (database me check krne se phle confirm kardiya ) ( iskeliye toh block hona hi chaiye ) 

🔸 Example (Wrong Use Case)

  • User registration:
    1. DB में data save करना
    2. Success message भेजना

👉 अगर async किया:

  • Message पहले चला गया
  • DB fail हो गया 😬

🔸 Correct Approach:

  • पहले DB save complete करो (blocking style)
  • फिर message भेजो

🔥 Conclusion (Part 1)

  • JS = synchronous + single-threaded
  • Blocking vs Non-blocking depends on use-case
  • Async code = performance improve करने का तरीका
  • लेकिन blindly use नहीं करना

🔹 9. JavaScript Engine क्या होता है?

👉 JS Engine = वो component जो JS code execute करता है

इसके main parts:

  1. Memory Heap
    • Variables store होते हैं
  2. Call Stack
    • Functions execute होते हैं

➡️ यही actual “JavaScript Engine” है

🔹 10. Problem कहाँ आती है?

👉 JS engine:

  • Single-threaded है
  • सब कुछ खुद नहीं कर सकता

👉 इसलिए:

  • External help लेता है (Browser / Node.js)

🔹 11. Web APIs क्या होते हैं?

👉 ये features JS के नहीं होते, बल्कि Browser provide करता है

🔸 Examples:

  • setTimeout()
  • setInterval()
  • DOM events (click, load, etc.)
  • fetch()

👉 Important:

  • Browser में → Web APIs
  • Node.js में → APIs (but DOM नहीं होता)
task quueue bhi hota h voh bhi javascrupt ko fast or asynchronus bnata h 

🔹 12. Complete Async System (Big Picture)

Async system में ये चीजें होती हैं:

  1. Call Stack
  2. Web APIs
  3. Task Queue
  4. Event Loop
agar m chahta hu ki kuch aisa ho mera kaam kre or us kaam ko ;hone ke baadd yaad dilade yeh isis se hota h 

🔹 13. Flow of Async Code (Step-by-Step)

🔸 Step 1: Code Call Stack में आता है

  • JS line-by-line execute करता है

🔸 Step 2: Async function detect होता है

👉 Example:

do side baad hello dena h yeh register call back me store hojata h ; isse kya hoga ki ; 

voh rewgister krtta h ise 2 second baad cla krna ise tb krna jb button dabe aise aise

setTimeout(() => {
  console.log("Hello");
}, 2000);

➡️ ये call stack से हटकर Web API में चला जाता है

🔸 Step 3: Web API handle करता है

  • Timer start होता है (2 sec)
  • JS engine free हो जाता है

🔸 Step 4: Callback ready होने पर

  • Callback → Task Queue में जाता है

🔸 Step 5: Event Loop check करता है

👉 Event Loop का काम:

  • Call Stack empty है या नहीं check करना

🔸 Step 6: Execution

  • अगर Call Stack empty है:
    • Task Queue से callback उठाकर execute करता है
    • yeh queue line me lgati h jo jo kaam hone wale hote h 
    • task queue saari chije call stack me daaldeta h 

🔹 14. Task Queue (Callback Queue)

👉 Queue structure (FIFO)

  • First In → First Out

👉 Example:

  • McDonald's line 🍔
  • पहले आया → पहले serve

🔹 15. Event Loop (Most Important 🔥)

👉 Event Loop = brain of async system

इसका काम:

  • Continuously check करना:
    • Call Stack empty है या नहीं
  • अगर empty है:
    • Task Queue से function उठाओ
    • Execute करो

🔹 16. Example Flow (Important)

console.log(1);

setTimeout(() => { ( set time out likht e hi call bnek web api me chlagya iske wjh se queue me yeh 3 ke baad lgega 
  console.log(2);
}, 0);

console.log(3);

🔸 Step-by-step execution:

  1. console.log(1) → print → ✅
  2. setTimeout → Web API में चला गया
  3. console.log(3) → print → ✅
  4. Callback → Task Queue में आया
  5. Event Loop → Call Stack empty देखा
  6. Callback execute → 2 print

✅ Final Output:

1
3
2

🔥 Important Trick Question

👉 setTimeout(..., 0) का मतलब:

  • तुरंत execute ❌
  • minimum delay के बाद execute ✔️

➡️ क्योंकि:

  • पहले पूरा call stack empty होगा

🔹 17. Callback Registration

👉 जब तुम async काम करते हो:

  • तुम actually callback register कर रहे होते हो

Examples:

  • setTimeout
  • setInterval
  • button click
  • event listeners

🔹 18. Task Queue vs Call Stack

FeatureCall StackTask Queue
StructureStack (LIFO)Queue (FIFO)
कामExecute codeWait for turn

📘 JavaScript Promises Notes (Hinglish) — Part 1

🔹 Promise Basics (Recap)

  • Promise = future me hone wala kaam
  • 2 main outcomes:
    • ✅ resolve → kaam successful
    • ❌ reject → error / failure

🔹 Promise banane ka basic syntax

const promiseThree = new Promise(function(resolve, reject){
    // async kaam
});
  • resolve → success ka signal
  • reject → error ka signal
  • Ye dono callback ke parameters hote hain

🔹 resolve ke andar data kaise bhejte hain

👉 Important concept:

resolve({username: "chai", email: "chai@example.com"});
  • resolve ke andar data pass kar sakte ho
  • mostly object pass hota hai (real world me)

🔹 Promise consume kaise hota hai (.then)

promiseThree.then(function(user){
    console.log(user);
});

👉 Samajh lo:

  • resolve() jo value bhejta hai
  • wahi .then() ke parameter me milti hai

✔ Flow:

resolve(data) → then(function(data))

🔹 Example Flow

const promiseThree = new Promise(function(resolve, reject){
    setTimeout(function(){
        resolve({username: "chai", email: "chai@example.com"});
    }, 1000);
});

promiseThree.then(function(user){
    console.log(user);
});

Output:

{username: "chai", email: "chai@example.com"}

🔹 Important Understanding

  • Promise async hota hai → delay ho sakta hai
  • .then() tabhi chalega jab resolve call hoga
  • resolve ke andar jo bhi bhejoge → wahi milega

🔹 Key Point

👉 Promise ka connection:

resolve()  👉  .then()
reject()   👉  .catch()

🔹 Next Concept Intro

  • Ab next me seekhenge:
    • Error handling (reject)
    • .catch()
    • chaining (.then ke baad .then)

🔹 Promise with Error Handling (resolve vs reject)

👉 Ab hum ek naya promise bana rahe hain jisme error bhi ho sakta hai

const promiseFour = new Promise(function(resolve, reject){
    setTimeout(function(){
        let error = true;

        if(!error){
            resolve({username: "hitesh", password: "123"});
        } else {
            reject("Something went wrong");
        }
    }, 1000);
});

🔹 Samajhne wali baat

  • error = true → reject chalega
  • error = false → resolve chalega

🔹 Promise Consume (then + catch)

promiseFour
.then(function(user){
    console.log(user);
})
.catch(function(error){
    console.log(error);
});

🔹 Flow samjho

resolve() → .then()
reject()  → .catch()

🔹 Output cases

✅ Case 1: error = false

{username: "hitesh", password: "123"}

❌ Case 2: error = true

Something went wrong

🔹 Promise Chaining (Very Important 🔥)

👉 .then() ke baad aur .then() laga sakte ho

promiseFour
.then(function(user){
    console.log(user);
    return user.username;
})
.then(function(username){
    console.log(username);
})
.catch(function(error){
    console.log(error);
});

🔹 Samajh lo chaining

  • Pehla .then() → full object milega
  • usme se value return karoge
  • next .then() → wahi return value milegi

🔹 Flow Diagram

resolve(data)
   ↓
then(user)
   ↓ return user.username
then(username)

🔹 Important Point

👉 .then() me jo return hota hai:

  • wo next .then() me pass hota hai

🔹 Common Mistake ❌

const username = promiseFour.then(...)
console.log(username);

❌ Ye kaam nahi karega
✔ Kyun?

  • Promise async hai → turant value nahi milti
  • result pending hota hai

🔹 finally() Concept

👉 finally() always run hota hai

chahe error aaye ya nhi ayae finally humehsa chlega

promise is eihter resolved or rejected 

promiseFour
.then(...)
.catch(...)
.finally(function(){
    console.log("Promise completed");
});

🔹 Important

  • resolve ho ya reject
  • finally() hamesha chalega

New Concept: async / await

promise ko humesha .then or .cathc se handle krna jrurui nhi h hum ise async or await se bhi handle karkskte h

👉 Promise ko handle karne ka modern & clean तरीका

  • .then/.catch ka alternative
  • readable code likhne ke liye use hota hai

🔹 Basic Syntax

async function consumePromiseFive() {
    const response = await promiseFive;
    console.log(response);
}

🔹 Important Keywords

✅ async (thodi der wait krt ah ki kaam hone ka)

  • function ko async banata hai
  • matlab → andar promises handle honge

✅ await (async jha likh diya wha await likhdet eh andar ki wait karte raho )

  • promise ke resolve hone ka wait karta hai
  • bina resolve hue aage nahi badhta

🔹 Real Example

const promiseFive = new Promise(function(resolve, reject){
    setTimeout(function(){
        let error = false;

        if(!error){
            resolve({username: "javascript", password: "123"});
        } else {
            reject("ERROR: JS went wrong");
        }
    }, 1000);
});

🔹 Using async/await

async function consumePromiseFive() {
    const response = await promiseFive;
    console.log(response);
}

consumePromiseFive();
bina async await ko krenge toh erorr ayega

🔹 Output

{username: "javascript", password: "123"}

🔹 Problem ⚠️

👉 Agar error aaya toh?

  • async/await direct error handle nahi karta
  • crash ho sakta hai
  • async await error handle nhi krskta islieye isme apko try and catch lgana hota h 

🔹 Solution: try...catch

async function consumePromiseFive() {
    try {
        const response = await promiseFive;
        console.log(response);
    } catch (error) {
        console.log(error);
    }
}

🔹 Flow samjho

await promise → resolve → try block
await promise → reject → catch block

🔹 Important Difference

Featurethen/catchasync/await
Syntaxchainingclean & readable
Error handle.catch()try/catch
Readabilitymediumhigh 🔥

🔹 Important Notes

  • await sirf async function ke andar hi use hota hai
  • ye code ko synchronous jaisa bana deta hai (but actually async hi hota hai)

🔹 Real World Use

👉 Mostly use hota hai:

  • API calls
  • database calls
  • file system

🔹 Next Level Concept Intro 🚀

Ab hum dekhenge:

  • fetch() API
  • real data kaise aata hai internet se
  • JSON handle kaise karte hain

🔹 Fetch API kya hai?

  • fetch() ek function hai jo network request karta hai
  • ye Promise return karta hai

👉 मतलब:

fetch() → Promise return karega

🔹 Basic Syntax

fetch("https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users")

🔹 Async/Await ke saath Fetch

async function getAllUsers() {
    try {
        const response = await fetch("https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users");
        const data = await response.json();
        console.log(data);
    } catch (error) {
        console.log("Error:", error);
    }
}

getAllUsers();

🔹 Step-by-Step Breakdown 🧠

1️⃣ fetch call

const response = await fetch(URL);
  • network request bhejta hai
  • response ek object hota hai

2️⃣ JSON conversion (VERY IMPORTANT ⚠️)

const data = await response.json();

👉 Important:

  • response.json() bhi Promise return karta hai
  • isliye usko bhi await karna पड़ता है

🔹 Common Mistake ❌

const data = response.json();
console.log(data);

❌ Galat hai
✔ Kyun?

  • ye Promise print karega (actual data nahi)

🔹 Correct Way ✅

const data = await response.json();

🔹 Important Insight 🔥

👉 Ye bahut log miss karte hain:

fetch() → async
response.json() → bhi async

👉 Dono ko await karna जरूरी hai

🔹 Flow Diagram

fetch() 
  ↓
response (Promise resolved)
  ↓
response.json()
  ↓
actual data

🔹 Same kaam using .then()

fetch("https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users")
.then(function(response){
    return response.json();
})
.then(function(data){
    console.log(data);
})
.catch(function(error){
    console.log(error);
});

🔹 Chaining samjho

  • pehla .then() → response
  • doosra .then() → actual data

🔹 finally() with fetch

fetch("https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users")
.then(...)
.catch(...)
.finally(function(){
    console.log("Request completed");
});

🔹 Important Realization 🤯

👉 Even JSON conversion bhi async hai
👉 Matlab simple kaam bhi async ho sakta hai

🔹 Advanced Insight (Behind the Scene Intro)

👉 Aapne dekha hoga:

  • fetch wala code kabhi kabhi pehle execute ho jata hai
  • humne fetch wala code likha toh baadme h but yeh sbse phle chl rha h kyuki isse phel toh saara sync h 
  • even agar niche likha ho

👉 Reason:

  • JavaScript ka event loop + async behavior
  • promises background me execute hote hain

👉 Isko next level pe detail me samjhenge (event loop)

🔹 Summary (Part 4)

  • fetch → network request
  • response.json() → data conversion
  • dono async hai
  • async/await ya then/catch dono use kar sakte ho

🔥 Final Understanding (Till Now)

Ab aap:

  • Promise bana sakte ho ✅
  • chaining kar sakte ho ✅
  • error handle kar sakte ho ✅
  • async/await use kar sakte ho ✅
  • fetch se real data la sakte ho ✅

🧩 Object Oriented Programming (OOP) + JS Reality

🧠 1. OOP क्या है?

👉 OOP = Programming Paradigm (Style)

मतलब:

  • Code लिखने का तरीका / style

Types of Programming Styles:

  • Object Oriented
  • Functional
  • Procedural

👉 JavaScript में ये सिर्फ style है, rule नहीं

❓ 2. क्या JavaScript में Classes होती हैं?

👉 Answer थोड़ा tricky है:

✔️ Short Answer:

हाँ (ES6 के बाद)

❗ Real Answer:

👉 Actually NO (internally)

🔥 3. Reality of JavaScript

👉 JavaScript है:

  • Prototype-based language

👉 NOT:

  • Pure class-based (like Java, C++)

🍬 4. Classes in JS = Syntactic Sugar

👉 मतलब:

  • Classes सिर्फ आसान syntax हैं
  • Behind the scenes:
    👉 Prototype ही चलता है

🧠 5. Important Concept (VERY IMPORTANT)

👉 याद रखो:

  • Class = Fake layer 😄
  • Prototype = Real system

📦 6. Object क्या होता है?

👉 Simple definition:

Object = Collection of:

  • Properties (variables)
  • Methods (functions)

🧾 Example:

const user = {
  name: "Rohit",
  age: 20,
  greet: function() {
    console.log("Hello")
  }
}

👉 यहाँ:

  • name, age → properties
  • greet → method

🧱 7. Object = Building Block

👉 JavaScript में:

  • Almost सब कुछ object है

Examples:

  • Arrays
  • Functions
  • Promises

❓ 8. OOP की जरूरत क्यों पड़ी?

👉 Problem:

  • Code messy हो रहा था
  • Reusability कम थी
  • “Spaghetti Code” बन रहा था 🍝

👉 Solution:

  • OOP आया → Structure better हुआ

🧩 9. OOP के Main Concepts (4 Pillars)

1. Abstraction

👉 Internal details hide करना

Example:

  • Fetch API
    • अंदर क्या हो रहा है → hidden
    • हमें बस use करना है

2. Encapsulation

👉 Data wrap करना + restrict access

  • कुछ चीजें accessible
  • कुछ hidden

3. Inheritance

👉 Properties inherit करना (parent → child)

👉 JS में ये:

  • Prototype से होता है

4. Polymorphism

👉 Same method → different behavior

Example:

  • toString()
  • map()

👉 ये 4 pillars:

  • हर language में अलग तरह से implement होते हैं

👉 JS में:

new keyword

📘 JavaScript Notes – Part 4

🏗️ Constructor Function + new keyword + this

🧠 1. Problem (Why Constructor Function?)

👉 Object Literal से problem:

const user1 = { name: "A", age: 20 }
const user2 = { name: "B", age: 25 }

❌ बार-बार same structure लिखना पड़ता है
❌ Reusability कम

💡 2. Solution → Constructor Function

👉 Constructor Function = Blueprint / Template

🧾 3. Example

function User(username, loginCount, isLoggedIn) {
  this.username = username
  this.loginCount = loginCount
  this.isLoggedIn = isLoggedIn
}

👉 यहाँ:

  • this → current object को refer करता है

⚡ 4. Object Create कैसे करें?

👉 new keyword use होता है:

const userOne = new User("hitesh", 10, true)
const userTwo = new User("rohit", 5, false)

🔍 5. new keyword internally क्या करता है? (VERY IMPORTANT 🔥)

जब आप लिखते हो:

new User(...)

Internally steps:

  1. 🆕 नया empty object बनता है
  2. 🔗 Prototype link होता है
  3. 📌 this उस object को point करता है
  4. 🧠 Function execute होता है
  5. 📦 Object return होता है

🧠 6. this keyword क्या है?

👉 this = current context

Constructor में:

  • this → new object

❗ 7. Without new क्या होगा?

const user = User("hitesh", 10, true)

❌ Problem:

  • this → global object को point करेगा
  • values गलत जगह store होंगी

👉 इसलिए:
✅ हमेशा new use करो

🧩 8. Method add करना

function User(username) {
  this.username = username
  
  this.greet = function() {
    console.log("Hello " + this.username)
  }
}

⚠️ 9. Problem with this approach

👉 हर object के साथ function duplicate बनेगा

user1.greet !== user2.greet

❌ Memory waste

💡 10. Solution → Prototype

👉 Methods prototype में डालो (next part में detail)

🔍 11. Check Constructor

console.log(userOne.constructor)

👉 Output:

  • function User()

🧠 12. instanceof

userOne instanceof User // true

👉 Check करता है:

  • object किससे बना है

📦 13. Real-world Understanding

👉 Constructor Function:

  • जैसे factory 🏭

👉 new:

  • machine start करता है

👉 Object:

  • final product

🔥 14. Key Takeaways

  • Constructor = reusable template
  • new = object creation engine
  • this = current object
  • बिना new = bug ❌

🔚 Part 4 Summary

  • Constructor Function समझा
  • new keyword internal working
  • this keyword clear
  • next step ready → Prototype

👉 Next Part (MOST IMPORTANT 🔥):

  • Prototype
  • Prototype Chain
  • Real inheritance in JS

🧬 Prototype + Prototype Chain (Core of JS)

🧠 1. Prototype क्या है?

👉 Simple शब्दों में:

Prototype = Object का backup / shared storage

👉 जहाँ common methods store होते हैं

❓ 2. Prototype की जरूरत क्यों?

👉 Problem (previous part से):

  • हर object में function duplicate बन रहा था ❌

👉 Solution:

  • Function को prototype में store करो ✅

🧾 3. Example

function User(username) {
  this.username = username
}

👉 Method prototype में add करो:

User.prototype.greet = function() {
  console.log("Hello " + this.username)
}

⚡ 4. Object create

const user1 = new User("hitesh")
const user2 = new User("rohit")

👉 अब:

  • greet method shared है ✅
  • memory बची ✅

🔍 5. Internally क्या होता है?

👉 जब आप:

user1.greet()

JS steps:

  1. Object में ढूंढेगा → ❌ नहीं मिला
  2. Prototype में जाएगा → ✅ मिल गया

🔗 6. Prototype Link

👉 हर object के अंदर hidden link होता है:

__proto__

👉 ये point करता है:

  • constructor के prototype को

🧬 7. Prototype Chain

👉 अगर method नहीं मिला:

  1. Object में check
  2. Prototype में check
  3. उसके prototype में check
  4. ...
  5. null तक

👉 इसे कहते हैं:
🔥 Prototype Chain

🧾 8. Diagram समझो

user1 → User.prototype → Object.prototype → null

🧠 9. Important Points

  • JS = Prototype-based language
  • Inheritance = Prototype chain से होता है

🧩 10. Real Inheritance Example

const user = {
  name: "hitesh"
}

const teacher = {
  subject: "JS"
}

teacher.__proto__ = user

👉 अब:

console.log(teacher.name) // hitesh

⚠️ 11. Modern तरीका

👉 __proto__ use avoid करो

Use:

Object.setPrototypeOf(child, parent)

🧠 12. Everything is Object

👉 JS में:

  • Array → Object
  • Function → Object
  • String → Object

🧪 13. Fun Example

Array.prototype.myMethod = function() {
  console.log("Custom method")
}

👉 अब हर array use कर सकता है:

[1,2,3].myMethod()

🔥 14. Important Insight

👉 Prototype = JS का backbone

👉 Classes भी internally:

  • Prototype ही use करती हैं

🧠 15. this + Prototype combo

👉 जब method prototype में होता है:

  • this still current object को refer करता है

🔚 Part 5 Summary

  • Prototype = shared storage
  • Duplicate code problem solve
  • Prototype chain = inheritance
  • JS का core concept clear

📘 JavaScript Notes – Part 6 (Final)

🏛️ Classes in JS + Reality Behind Scenes

🧠 1. Classes क्या होती हैं?

👉 ES6 (2015) में introduce हुई

👉 Syntax easy बनाने के लिए

🧾 2. Basic Example

class User {
  constructor(username, loginCount, isLoggedIn) {
    this.username = username
    this.loginCount = loginCount
    this.isLoggedIn = isLoggedIn
  }

  greet() {
    console.log("Hello " + this.username)
  }
}

⚡ 3. Object Create करना

const user1 = new User("hitesh", 10, true)

🔍 4. Compare with Constructor Function

👉 Same चीज पहले ऐसे लिखते थे:

function User(username) {
  this.username = username
}

User.prototype.greet = function() {
  console.log("Hello " + this.username)
}

💡 5. Key Insight (VERY IMPORTANT 🔥)

👉 Class = सिर्फ shortcut है

👉 Behind the scenes:

  • Prototype ही use हो रहा है

🧠 6. Class internally क्या करती है?

जब आप class लिखते हो:

  1. Function बनता है
  2. Prototype create होता है
  3. Methods prototype में जाते हैं
  4. new से object बनता है

🧩 7. Constructor क्या है?

👉 Special method:

constructor() {}
  • Automatically call होता है
  • Object initialize करता है

🧠 8. Methods कहाँ जाते हैं?

👉 Class के methods:

  • Prototype में store होते हैं

👉 इसलिए:

  • Memory efficient ✅

🔒 9. Strict Mode

👉 Classes हमेशा:

  • Strict mode में run होती हैं

⚠️ 10. Important Rules

  • Class को बिना new call नहीं कर सकते ❌ 
User() ❌
  • Always: 
new User() ✅

🧬 11. Inheritance with Classes

class User {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name
  }
}

class Admin extends User {
  constructor(name, role) {
    super(name)
    this.role = role
  }
}

🔍 12. super keyword

👉 Parent constructor call करता है

🧠 13. Behind the Scene (IMPORTANT 🔥)

👉 ये class actually convert होती है:

function Admin() {}
Admin.prototype = Object.create(User.prototype)

👉 मतलब:

  • Prototype chain बनती है

🔁 14. Full Flow (Big Picture)

  1. Class लिखा
  2. JS ने function बनाया
  3. Prototype attach हुआ
  4. new → object बना
  5. this → object को point किया
  6. methods → prototype से आए

🔥 15. Final Understanding

👉 JavaScript:

  • ❌ Pure OOP नहीं
  • ❌ Pure Class-based नहीं

👉 ✅ Prototype-based है
